title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Raymond Weitekamp discusses how recursive language model patterns at inference time can be mapped onto coding agents to improve performance and reliability. The approach uses recursive computation patterns to enhance agent behavior on code-generation tasks.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse maps recursive language model patterns used at inference time onto coding agents, with the goal of improving performance and reliability on code-generation tasks. The talk frames recursive computation as a way to shape agent behavior rather than relying on a single forward pass. The concepts in scope include test-time compute, local models, and AI agents.
